title: Generation Speed Compared
concept: GANs generate instantly in one pass; VAEs are also fast; Diffusion models are traditionally much slower, requiring many sequential denoising steps.

exercises.prompt: If you wanted a picture RIGHT NOW with zero waiting, which type of AI model would you want to use?

options.text: One that draws the whole picture in a single instant snap
One that redraws the picture 1000 times, slowly cleaning it up
One that takes a coffee break between every brushstroke

exercises.explain: A single-snap generator is way faster than a model that needs hundreds of slow cleanup steps!

cards.title: Snap vs. Slow-Cook
cards.body: Some AI models create images like a camera flash — instantly! Others create images like a slow-cooked stew — bit by bit, over many steps. Today we compare the SPEED of GANs, VAEs, and Diffusion models.

cards.title: Speed Showdown
cards.body: GANs generate an image in ONE quick pass — the generator just outputs a finished image instantly. VAEs are also fast, decoding a single point into an image in one shot. Diffusion models are traditionally much slower — they start with pure noise and clean it up over MANY repeated steps before the image looks good.

cards.title: Instant Noodles vs. Slow Stew
cards.body: A GAN or VAE is like instant noodles — pour water, done in minutes! A Diffusion model is like a slow-cooked stew — it needs many rounds of simmering and stirring before it's ready to eat.

cards.title: Real-Time Game Avatars
cards.body: A video game that creates a new character face for you WHILE you play needs speed — it uses a GAN or VAE for instant results. If it used old-school diffusion with hundreds of steps, you'd be stuck staring at a loading screen!

cards.title: Myth: Diffusion Is Always Slow Forever
cards.body: Diffusion models USED to always need hundreds of steps, making them slow. But newer tricks have sped many of them up a lot. Still, in their classic/traditional form, diffusion is much slower than the one-shot speed of GANs and VAEs.

cards.title: One Step vs. Many Steps
cards.body: Compare how each model reaches a finished image.

left.text: One quick pass — image appears almost instantly

right.text: Many repeated denoising steps — slow, step-by-step cleanup

exercises.prompt: Which model type traditionally needs many sequential steps to generate one image?
exercises.explain: Diffusion models traditionally clean up noise over many steps, unlike the one-pass speed of GANs and VAEs.

exercises.prompt: True or False: GANs generate an image in a single instant pass.
exercises.explain: True! The GAN's generator produces a finished image in one quick pass, no repeated steps needed.

exercises.sentence: Diffusion models traditionally need many sequential ___ steps to clean an image from noise.
exercises.explain: Diffusion models gradually remove noise across many denoising steps, which takes more time than a one-shot generator.

exercises.prompt: You need to generate 1,000 images as fast as possible for a mobile app. Which model type is the better traditional choice for speed?

options.text: GAN, because it creates each image in one quick pass
Traditional diffusion, because it repeats denoising many times
It doesn't matter, all models take the same time

exercises.explain: GANs (and VAEs) generate images in a single fast pass, making them a better fit when speed matters most, compared to traditional slow diffusion.

exercises.prompt: A student says: 'All AI image generators take the exact same amount of time to make a picture.' What's wrong with this statement?

options.text: Nothing — all models really are equally fast
GANs and VAEs generate in one fast pass, while traditional diffusion models need many slow, sequential denoising steps
Diffusion models are actually the fastest of all
Speed depends only on the size of the picture, not the model type

exercises.explain: The model's design matters a lot! GANs and VAEs create images instantly in one pass, while traditional diffusion models take much longer because they repeat a denoising process many times.
